question: Based on the quarterly platform engagement distribution, what can be concluded about 'Page Views' over the year from this chart alone?

options.A: The chart only shows the share of page views each quarter, so it does not show whether the absolute number of page views is increasing or decreasing.
options.B: The absolute number of page views increases each quarter because their share of the total is larger in later quarters.
options.C: The absolute number of page views decreases each quarter because their share of the total is smaller in later quarters.
options.D: The absolute number of page views stays the same across all quarters because each bar reaches 100%.

explanation: The chart normalizes unrelated categories to 100% within each quarter. It can suggest that the share of a metric is higher in later quarters, but it cannot establish an absolute increasing trend without the underlying totals. A higher percentage share does not necessarily mean a higher absolute quantity.
